question.label: Direction
question.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
question.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

answer: I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.

assessment.note: He names delegation as the lever to create room for bigger scope, which shows growth intent and ownership, with a mild sustainability strain implied by current hands-on load. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'delegate more of the hands-on work' is a concrete thread to pick up next session.

new_queue.label: Delegation boundary
new_queue.name: What work are you still holding that should probably sit with others?

new_queue.label: First growth move
new_queue.name: Given what we’ve covered, where do you want to focus first?

history.answer_excerpt: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
